ea_init_pointer.preamble:
EA-10 is the first EA spawned for Stage 6 (Hardening). Stage 6 is LOW risk and may span multiple EA-10 sessions. This Phase 1 dispatch covers the quick-wins batch ONLY: 1 ticket close (KK auto-resolved), 11 ticket retirements (Defunct label + complete), 1 governance policy doc (OO), 1 STATUS.md anomaly entry (A32), and 1 single-line CLAUDE.md label inventory addition. Subsequent phases (2 = Items 6.4 + 6.5; 3 = HIGH-risk II/TT/JJ; 4 = posterity; 5 = doc cleanup; 6 = investigations; 7 = automation; 8 = G hygiene; 9 = UU; 10 = archival; 11 = Stage 6 close) will be authored as separate init pointers per phase.
LA explicitly approved this dispatch on 2026-04-30 post-Triage-Report review. EA-10 must execute the entirety of Phase 1 in a single session and stop at Phase 1 close — do NOT proceed to Phase 2 without a fresh dispatch.

comprehension_gate.rule: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION. Before executing any work item, present a structured comprehension-gate summary back to the LA via chat (NOT to a file). Wait for Guide-#10 review (the LA will paste the summary back to Guide-#10 for verdict). Do NOT execute any work until Guide-#10 returns APPROVED via the LA.

required_summary_sections.section: **Stage objective restatement** (in your own words; verify alignment with LA's Phase 1 scope above)
**Work item enumeration** — all 5 items by ID and one-line scope each (6.7.5.1 KK close; 6.7.5.2 placeholder retirement; 6.7.5.3 OO policy doc; 6.7.5.4 STATUS A32; 6.7.5.5 CLAUDE.md label addition)

**Files to be touched** — exhaustive list (BlarAI-side: STATUS.md, CLAUDE.md; devplatform-side: 1 new file in devplatform/docs/governance/; Vikunja-side: 12 ticket mutations + 1 label-application sequence per ticket)
**Verification strategy** — commit-by-commit diff readback + Vikunja-state read-back (project_summary on Project 10 + get_task on each modified ticket)

**Risk acknowledgment** — name the dominant risks for Phase 1 (V8 SOFTENED via LA explicit-authorization for CLAUDE.md single-line edit; w11/dn14 EA-paraphrase pattern; Defunct label idempotency under retry)

**Rollback awareness** — `git revert` on each commit + Vikunja: remove Defunct label + reopen ticket via update_task. No HALT-gate equivalents to Stages 2-5 in Phase 1; rollback is per-commit reversible.

**Pre-Flight discipline acknowledgment** — explicit commitment to run pause-fleet (state.pause_fleet) THEN commit BEFORE any branch checkout or substantive edit, per copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop. Phase 1 stays on main branch; no checkout needed; pause-then-commit still required.

**Cumulative-ack chain inheritance restatement** — the 132-atom inheritance chain by name; do NOT re-enumerate every atom but acknowledge the inheritance + state new acks will use g10-ea10_n* namespace

pre_flight_step_0_rollback_readiness.intro: LA-directive 2026-04-30: Stage 6 is non-destructive and reversible per-commit. The 5-check rollback-readiness preflight from Stage 5 (which protected against irreversible deletion) scales down to 4 checks for Stage 6. Any FAIL = HARD STOP; report to LA via chat with the failed-check evidence and AWAIT direction. Do NOT proceed past Step 0 if any check fails.

check.name: .platform/ contents preserved
check.command: Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.platform -Force | Select-Object Name | Sort-Object Name
check.expected: 6 entries: active_tasks.yaml, audit_archive, docs_sprints, scheduled, sprints, vikunja_project_ids.yaml
check.reasoning: Stage 5 moved BlarAI-side platform-coupling artifacts into .platform/. Phase 1 must NOT touch .platform/; this check confirms Stage 5 close state is intact at session boot.

check.name: Three platform-extraction tags preserved
check.command: git -C C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI tag --list 'pre-platform-extract' 'post-platform-extract'; git -C C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform tag --list 'platform-extracted'
check.expected: 3 tags total: BlarAI: `pre-platform-extract` (annotates d0dc1c7) + `post-platform-extract` (annotates 4122b92); devplatform: `platform-extracted` (annotates ae75507)
check.reasoning: RECOVERY.md asset coverage matrix depends on these tags. Phase 1 must NOT delete or move tags; this check confirms rollback parachute integrity.

check.name: Worktrees clean on both repos
check.expected_blarai: Empty output (clean working tree at HEAD `fefb16a`).
check.expected_devplatform: 3 known carried-forward Stage-4-residue untracked items only: `?? docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md`, `?? tools/vikunja/config.yml.pre_stage4_bak`, `?? tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/inbox.json`. NO modified-files (M lines). NO other untracked entries beyond those 3.
check.reasoning: Phase 1 commits must be clean per Stage 4/5 commit-discipline precedent. Any modified files at boot indicate an in-flight edit from a prior session that must be resolved BEFORE Phase 1 begins.

check.name: state.json fleet_paused=False at boot
check.command: Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\autonomy_budget\state.json | ConvertFrom-Json | Select-Object fleet_paused, fleet_paused_reason, last_updated_by, last_updated_utc
check.expected: fleet_paused = False; fleet_paused_reason = $null; last_updated_by = "claude_code"; last_updated_utc set to a timestamp post-`2026-04-30T19:00:00Z` (Guide-#9 final unpause + subsequent Guide-#10 boot reads).
check.reasoning: EA-10 will pause-then-commit at Pre-Flight Step 1. If state.json shows `fleet_paused=true` at boot from an unknown owner, surface it to LA before pausing again — could indicate a prior in-flight pause that wasn't unpaused cleanly.

on_any_check_fail.action: Report failed check by ID + actual output + expected output to LA via chat. Do NOT proceed past Step 0. Do NOT execute pause-fleet, commits, or work items. Stand by for LA + Guide-#10 disposition.

pre_flight_step_1_pause_fleet.intro: Per CLAUDE.md §Fleet-Pause SOP + copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop: pause fleet FIRST, commit immediately, THEN proceed to substantive work. EA-10 stays on main throughout Phase 1 (no branch checkout).

step.name: pause_fleet
step.command: python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('Stage 6 Phase 1 quick-wins batch -- EA-10', updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')"
step.verify: fleet_paused=True; fleet_paused_reason="Stage 6 Phase 1 quick-wins batch -- EA-10"; last_updated_by="ea_code"; last_updated_utc refreshed.

step.name: commit_pause
step.command: cd C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform; git add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json; git commit -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 1 quick-wins batch -- EA-10"
step.expected: Single-file commit on devplatform main; capture short SHA for stage close report.

cumulative_ack_chain_inheritance.intro: EA-10 inherits the 132-atom cumulative-ack chain established through Stages 0-5. New EA-10 acks emit under namespace `g10-ea10_n*` per global EA-numbering convention (feedback memory: ea numbering is global, not stage-scoped). EA-10 = Stage 6 first EA, globally EA-10.

stage.origin: Stage 1-3 EA inheritance + Stage 3 close audit checkpoint

chain_summary.stage: g7-ea1_n10..n13 + g7-ea7_n14 (Stage 3 acks; n10..n13 are documentation-defect labels per A12 / Ticket A scope; verbatim per no-amend doctrine)

g8-ea8_n1(a..p) + g8-ea8_n2(a..f) + g8-ea8_n3(a..k) (Stage 4 preflight + workflow simplification + execution-time refinements; corrected from Guide-#9 5.14 handoff brief enumeration error of `n3(a..l)` — actual disk enumeration is a..k = 11 atoms. Underlying 5.14 handoff defect is committed audit artifact and remains unamended per dn32; defect folded into Ticket MM (Vikunja id 303) scope as an additional posterity-correction sub-item alongside the 3 already-pending — LA directive 2026-04-30. EA-10 does NOT update MM in Phase 1; the MM scope-expansion will be picked up by the EA executing MM in a future phase.)
g9-ea9_n1(a..n) + g9-ea9_n2(a) + g9-ea9_n2(b) (Stage 5 preflight + Phase E + Phase F mid-execution remediations)
chain_summary.total: 132 binding atoms at Stage 5 close (per `5.12-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_9_STAGE5_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml` + Guide-#9 → Guide-#10 handoff metadata)

cumulative_ack_chain_inheritance.restatement_requirement: EA-10's iter-1 comprehension-gate summary MUST acknowledge the 132-atom inheritance chain by name (per the chain_summary above) and explicitly state that any new acks emitted during Phase 1 will use `g10-ea10_n*` namespace. EA-10 does NOT need to re-enumerate every atom verbatim (that would bloat the gate). Acknowledgment-by-name + namespace-commitment suffices for LOW-risk Phase 1.

required_acknowledgments.intro: The following items must be explicitly acknowledged in EA-10's iter-1 comprehension-gate summary (verbatim or paraphrased; Guide-#10 will verify in gate review). Missing acknowledgment of any LOAD-BEARING item = REJECTED gate.
required_acknowledgments.acknowledge: I will run state.pause_fleet(...) THEN commit BEFORE any substantive work, per copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop. I will run state.resume_fleet(...) AND commit as the LAST action before exiting the session.
I will NOT call state.unpause_fleet — that function does NOT exist. The correct function name is state.resume_fleet (per V7 / GUIDE_PROMPT operating rule).
I will NOT merge to main; merge gating is exhausted at Stage 5.10 (post-completion). Phase 1 commits land directly on main per the post-Stage-5 single-repo flow (no chore/* branch).
I will NOT pop or apply ANY wake_launcher auto-stash entry per dn10 + w8 (corrected per A20). Discipline is generic regardless of timestamp.

I will NOT edit CLAUDE.md or .github/copilot-instructions.md as part of doctrine split (V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred). The single-line label-inventory addition to CLAUDE.md in Work Item 6.7.5.5 is LA-explicit-authorized (2026-04-30) as a trivial concurrent edit to keep label inventory in sync — NOT setting precedent for further CLAUDE.md edits and NOT a doctrine split. I will scope the edit to the single line specified.
I will NOT archive `docs/platform_separation/` to `docs/archive/` — Item 6.9 archival is DEFERRED to late-Stage-6 per d29 / dn33 (only after MM/SS/TT/UU complete).

For each of the 11 placeholder tickets (K, L, M, V, W, Y, Z, AA, BB, CC, EE — Vikunja IDs 284, 285, 286, 287, 288, 290, 291, 292, 293, 294, 295), I will execute the retirement procedure in this exact order: (a) add Vikunja comment with retirement text, (b) apply Defunct label (id 22) via add_label_to_task, (c) mark complete via complete_task. Order matters: comment + label BEFORE complete so the label is visible during the open→closed transition.

For KK (id 301), I will NOT apply the Defunct label — KK is genuinely auto-resolved by Stage 5.1 directory deletion, NOT retired due to unrecoverable scope. KK gets a verification-comment + complete_task only.

I will use mcp__vikunja__* tools (canonical Vikunja access pattern) for all Vikunja mutations; I will NOT fall back to direct REST API. For each mutation I will read back the resulting state via get_task or list_tasks before declaring the mutation successful.

I will NOT touch tickets X (id 289), I (id 283), FF (id 296), GG (id 297), HH (id 298), II (id 299), JJ (id 300), LL (id 302), MM (id 303), PP (id 305), QQ (id 306), RR (id 307), SS (id 308), TT (id 309), B-G (ids 310-315), UU (id 316), or A (id 282) during Phase 1 — those are out-of-scope for this batch.

I will read this entire init pointer + STATUS.md §6/§7/§8/§9 + 07_STAGE6_HARDENING.xml + GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml + the Triage Report content in this conversation BEFORE emitting the iter-1 comprehension-gate summary.
I will run the Pre-Flight Step 0 4-check verification BEFORE proceeding to Pre-Flight Step 1 pause. Any FAIL = HARD STOP per Step 0 directive.

item.name: KK close — autonomy_budget/state.json dead-artifact (Vikunja id 301)
item.description: Ticket KK was bound at Phase C Closure (2026-04-29) for cleanup of `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` as a dead artifact post-Phase-C4 (no longer read by wake_launcher; intentionally paused). Stage 5.1 deleted the parent directory `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/`, which auto-resolved this ticket without explicit cleanup action.

verify_auto_resolution.expected: False (file absent because parent dir absent).

vikunja_actions.action: mcp__vikunja__add_task_comment with task_id=301 and comment text: "Auto-resolved by Stage 5.1 directory deletion. Verified `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/` absent at <UTC-timestamp> via Test-Path. No explicit cleanup action was required; the parent-directory deletion in Stage 5.1 (commit 3e73484) removed both the directory and the dead state.json artifact in a single operation. KK closed without remediation work. Reference: Phase C Closure Summary; STATUS.md §6 line 390."
mcp__vikunja__get_task with task_id=301; verify done=true and label list does NOT include Defunct (label id 22 should NOT be applied).

item.no_label_distinction: KK does NOT receive the Defunct label. Defunct semantically means "no execution work performed; ticket retired due to unrecoverable scope." KK had a clearly-recoverable scope (the file existed; the cleanup was simply auto-completed by a deletion in a prior stage). The semantic distinction is LA-explicit per Phase 1 dispatch: Defunct ≠ auto-resolved.

item.name: 11-placeholder retirement (K/L/M/V/W/Y/Z/AA/BB/CC/EE) — Vikunja ids 284-288, 290-295
item.description: Per Guide-#10 Triage Report retire-vs-execute analysis (LA-approved 2026-04-30): 11 placeholder tickets enumerated as letter slots in Stage 4 close inheritance lists (4.13 handoff line 376; STATUS.md §6 line 387) have NO recoverable granular binding context in committed audit-chain artifacts. Retirement is the only honest disposition — fabricating scope from imagination is forbidden (dn14 / Ticket X / w11). LA-directive: apply Defunct label (id 22) AND mark Complete via complete_task. Both, not either-or. The Defunct label gives clean completion stats AND a visible audit marker that no work was actually performed.
item.note_on_excluded_id_289: Vikunja id 289 = X (subagent verification protocol formalization). X is NOT a placeholder; it has clear binding context (A18; HIGH priority per handoff). Do NOT touch id 289 in this batch.

per_ticket_procedure.step: mcp__vikunja__add_task_comment with the SAME comment text for all 11 tickets: "RETIRED 2026-04-30. No granular binding context recoverable in committed audit-chain artifacts (Guide-#10 one-pass search across Stages 0-3 dispatch + handoff XMLs + STATUS.md). Per d31 successor instruction + LA approval. Inheritance-list integrity preserved via Vikunja audit trail."
mcp__vikunja__add_label_to_task with task_id=<ticket_id> and label_id=22 (Defunct, color #9E9E9E gray).
mcp__vikunja__get_task with task_id=<ticket_id>; verify (a) done=true, (b) Defunct label (id 22) is in the task's label list, (c) the retirement comment is the most recent comment.

item.order_matters: Comment + label BEFORE complete (per LA directive 2026-04-30) so the label is visible during the open→closed transition rather than appearing post-close. Apply this order strictly per ticket; do NOT batch the label-applications after all tickets are completed.
item.idempotency_note: If an EA-10 retry occurs (e.g., session interruption + restart), the retry should be safe: comments are append-only (a duplicate retirement comment is harmless audit residue but not a correctness issue); add_label_to_task is idempotent on already-applied label; complete_task on already-complete task is idempotent. EA-10 should detect the partial-completion state via get_task readback before re-executing the procedure on a given ticket.

item.name: OO policy doc — auto-running reports / digests / dashboards (Vikunja id 304)
item.description: Ticket OO was bound at Stage 5 preflight (5.0.v2 audit checkpoint) for codification of the LA's directive that 5 fleet_observability tasks (Welcome Back Poll, Daily Digest, Weekly Summary, Dashboard Maintainer, Toast Watchdog) stay Disabled per Phase A 2026-04-29; re-enabling requires LA pre-approval. Reference: STATUS.md Phase C addendum line 483; 5.14 handoff successor instructions.

file_to_create.reasoning: Adjacent to existing fleet-hygiene.md governance doc; lives devplatform-side per d30 (governance/ split deferred — but this doc is BORN devplatform-side, not migrated from BlarAI). Filename in snake_case to match repo convention.

item.content_outline:
Markdown structure: ``` # Policy: No Auto-Running Reports / Digests / Dashboards Without LA Pre-Approval
**Effective**: 2026-04-29 (Phase A LA directive) **Scope**: Scheduled tasks under TaskPath `\BlarAI\` registered as auto-recurring report/digest/dashboard generators **Status**: ACTIVE
## Background At Stage 4 close (2026-04-28) the fleet had 13 active scheduled tasks. Phase A cost-mitigation review (2026-04-29) identified 5 tasks as auto-running reports/ digests/dashboards whose value LA did not recognize as ongoing-required. LA directive: disable those 5 tasks; do NOT re-enable without explicit LA pre-approval.
## The 5 Disabled Tasks - Welcome Back Poll - Daily Digest - Weekly Summary - Dashboard Maintainer - Toast Watchdog
## Policy 1. Any new scheduled task whose function is "auto-generate a report, digest, dashboard, or unsolicited summary" REQUIRES explicit LA pre-approval before registration. 2. Re-enabling any of the 5 disabled tasks above REQUIRES explicit LA pre-approval with a justification of recurring value. 3. Operationally-essential auto-running tasks (wake_launcher, agents-cadence-monitor, escalation_watchdog, sprint_auditor, gate_stale_cleaner, credentials_rotation_reminder) are NOT in scope of this policy — they are work-progressing tasks, not reporting tasks. 4. The line between "report" and "operational" is judgmental. Default: if LA does not routinely consult its output, treat it as a report and apply this policy.
## Audit Trail - 2026-04-29 Phase A LA directive — STATUS.md §"Phase C addendum" line ref - 2026-04-30 OO policy codification (this doc) — Stage 6.7.5 EA-10 Phase 1 ``` EA-10 may polish wording at execution time; the structure + content above is the spec.

vikunja_actions.action: mcp__vikunja__add_task_comment with task_id=304 and comment text: "Policy doc authored at devplatform/docs/governance/auto_running_reports_policy.md (commit <short-SHA>). Codifies LA directive 2026-04-29 (Phase A) that 5 disabled fleet_observability tasks remain disabled and re-enabling requires LA pre-approval. Reference: STATUS.md Phase C addendum + 5.14 handoff."

item.name: STATUS.md §9 anomaly entry A32 — placeholder retirement audit trail
item.description: Append a new anomaly entry A32 to STATUS.md §9 "Anomalies bound (A20-A31)" — section header should be updated to "Anomalies bound (A20-A32)". A32 documents the placeholder-retirement disposition for posterity.

file_to_edit.edit_location: Within "### 9. Anomalies bound (A20-A31)" section, after the A31 entry (currently the last line before "### 10. Stage 5 transitions to Stage 6"). Update the section header from "(A20-A31)" to "(A20-A32)".

item.a32_text: - **A32**: 11 Stage-4-cycle placeholder ticket bodies retired (2026-04-30, Guide-#10 + LA Phase 1 dispatch). Letters K, L, M, V, W, Y, Z, AA, BB, CC, EE (Vikunja IDs 284-288, 290-295) were enumerated as inheritance slots in the Stage 4 close backlog (4.13 handoff line 376; STATUS.md §6 line 387) but no granular binding context was preserved in committed audit-chain artifacts. Guide-#10 one-pass search across Stages 0-3 dispatch + handoff XMLs + STATUS.md returned zero recoverable scope. Retired via Defunct label (id 22, gray #9E9E9E) + complete_task per LA directive 2026-04-30. Both label-and-complete (not either-or): completion stats stay clean while the Defunct label provides a visible audit marker that no execution work was performed. Documentation-defect remediation, not hardening-optionality concession. Skip-the-cancelled-letter convention applies to any future backlog re-enumeration. Backlog count 35 → 24 active.
item.verification: After edit + commit, `git -C C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI show HEAD -- docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md` should show only the A32 addition + section-header update; no incidental edits elsewhere in STATUS.md.

item.name: CLAUDE.md label inventory single-line addition — Defunct label
item.description: LA-explicit-authorized 2026-04-30 (overrides V8 / 6.1.v2 default-prohibition for this single trivial edit ONLY): add a single line to CLAUDE.md's Vikunja Conventions § Labels block to keep the inventory in sync with the server's label list. NOT a doctrine split. NOT setting precedent for further CLAUDE.md edits.

file_to_edit.edit_location: Within "### Vikunja Conventions" → "Labels (server-canonical, verified 2026-04-20 via `list_labels`)" block. The existing block in CLAUDE.md lists 8 base labels (Active through Security) + 6 Gate:* labels (current server count to be verified at boot via list_labels; addition makes N+1). The single-line addition for Defunct should land just before the Gate:* group, after the 8 base labels. Format must match the surrounding pattern. Do NOT attempt to bring CLAUDE.md's inventory into full sync with the server (which has additional Status:*, Budget:*, Gate:Pending-Execution, Gate:Stale labels not in CLAUDE.md) — that broader sync is out-of-scope for Phase 1.
file_to_edit.verify_at_boot: EA-10's Pre-Flight Step 0 sequence may augment with `mcp__vikunja__list_labels` to read current label inventory and identify the precise insertion point. Use that read as authoritative; CLAUDE.md must reflect the server reality, not the spec.

item.addition_text_template: Match the existing 8-base-labels format exactly. Example pattern: `Defunct (id 22, gray ` + backtick + `#9E9E9E` + backtick + `).` Place on its own line within the 8-base-labels paragraph (before the Gate:* paragraph) so the inventory reads naturally — the precise placement is EA-10's call at execution time as long as it is BEFORE the Gate:* section per LA directive.
item.scope_constraint: STRICT: only add the Defunct line. Do NOT touch any other line in CLAUDE.md. Do NOT reformat the surrounding section. Do NOT add a comment block explaining the addition. Do NOT update other parts of CLAUDE.md (Active State / Phase History / etc.) even if drift is observed — that is out-of-scope for Phase 1.
item.v8_softening_note: V8 / dn14 default-prohibition on CLAUDE.md edits is SOFTENED for this single edit only via LA explicit authorization 2026-04-30. EA-10 must NOT generalize this softening to other CLAUDE.md edits in Phase 1 or any future phase without re-asking for explicit authorization.

commit_discipline.intro: Phase 1 produces 3 commits total across both repos. Single-commit-per-purpose precedent from Stage 4/5.

commit.message: chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 1 quick-wins batch -- EA-10
docs(governance): codify OO -- no auto-running reports/digests/dashboards without LA pre-approval

commit.files: docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md (Work Item 6.7.5.4 A32 entry); CLAUDE.md (Work Item 6.7.5.5 Defunct label inventory)
commit.message: docs(stage6_phase1): A32 placeholder retirement audit trail + CLAUDE.md Defunct label inventory addition
commit.bundling_rationale: Both edits are BlarAI-side single-line additions; bundling them in one commit keeps history clean. Alternative: split into 2 commits if EA-10 prefers strict-single-purpose-per-commit. EA-10's call.

commit.message: chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 1 quick-wins batch -- EA-10 done

commit_discipline.total: 4 commits (3 devplatform + 1 BlarAI). 12 Vikunja mutations (1 KK close + 11 placeholder retirements + 1 OO close = 13 ticket-state changes; note OO close is a comment + complete on a single ticket so 2 mutations; total Vikunja mutations: 1 KK comment + 1 KK complete + 11 × 3 placeholder steps (comment + label + complete) = 35 + 1 OO comment + 1 OO complete = 37 mutations).

verification_commands_for_la.intro: Commands the LA may run independently to verify Phase 1 close. Guide-#10 will verify these via cross-check at Phase 1 close gate review.
verification_commands_for_la.cmd: mcp__vikunja__get_task --task_id 284 (and inspect labels for id 22) ; same for ids 285, 286, 287, 288, 290, 291, 292, 293, 294, 295
mcp__vikunja__get_task --task_id 301 (verify labels list does NOT include id 22)

exit_criteria.criterion: Pre-Flight Step 0 4-check verification PASS at session boot.
Pre-Flight Step 1 pause + commit landed on devplatform main.
KK (id 301) closed via comment + complete_task; NO Defunct label applied.
All 11 placeholders (ids 284-288, 290-295) closed via comment + Defunct label (id 22) + complete_task IN THAT ORDER per ticket.
OO (id 304) policy doc authored at devplatform/docs/governance/auto_running_reports_policy.md + committed + ticket closed via comment + complete_task.
STATUS.md §9 anomaly section header updated A20-A31 → A20-A32; A32 entry appended with the prescribed text.
CLAUDE.md Vikunja-Conventions Labels block has the Defunct line added before the Gate:* section. No other CLAUDE.md edits.
Post-Flight unpause + commit landed on devplatform main; state.json fleet_paused=False at session exit.
EA-10 emits a Phase 1 close report listing all 4 commit SHAs + Vikunja mutation enumeration + readback evidence for each ticket.
Guide-#10 receives the close report and runs Phase 1 close-verification cross-check (git diff readback + Vikunja get_task on all 13 affected tickets) before authorizing Phase 2 spawn.

post_flight_step_unpause_fleet.intro: LAST action before EA-10 session exit. Per fleet-pause SOP §3.

step.name: commit_unpause
step.command: cd C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform; git add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json; git commit -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Stage 6 Phase 1 quick-wins batch -- EA-10 done"
step.expected: Single-file commit on devplatform main; capture short SHA for stage close report.

post_flight_step_unpause_fleet.function_name_reminder: resume_fleet, NOT unpause_fleet — calling state.unpause_fleet raises AttributeError per V7 + ack2.

vikunja_workflow_conventions.intro: Per CLAUDE.md §Vikunja Conventions + LA directive 2026-04-29 (feedback_doc_cleanup_non_optional). Phase 1 mutations across 13 tickets must follow these conventions exactly.
vikunja_workflow_conventions.convention: Use mcp__vikunja__* tools for ALL mutations (canonical Vikunja access pattern); do NOT fall back to REST API.
For each mutation, immediately read back via get_task or list_tasks before declaring success. Trust no claim without disk verification (per dn14 / A18 / w11).
Comments are append-only; if a retry is needed, do NOT delete prior comment — just add a new one explaining the retry.
Defunct label (id 22) is RESERVED for "no execution work performed; ticket retired due to unrecoverable scope." Do NOT apply it to KK, OO, or any non-placeholder ticket in Phase 1.
complete_task is the canonical close mechanism; it sets done=true. Do NOT attempt to "close" via update_task with a status field — that pattern is not the canonical convention.

hard_rules_and_standing_reminders.rule: If any work item fails (e.g., add_label_to_task returns an error for a ticket), STOP that ticket's procedure, document the failure, and report to LA via chat. Do NOT proceed to next ticket until disposition received.
If Pre-Flight Step 0 detects ANY check fail, HARD STOP per Step 0 directive. Do NOT pause the fleet, do NOT execute any work item.
If during execution an unexpected file modification appears (e.g., git status shows an unexpected M line), STOP and report. Do NOT add unintended files to the commit.

Phase 1 close report MUST include the 4 commit SHAs + Vikunja mutation enumeration + readback evidence + Pre-Flight Step 0 outputs + Post-Flight unpause confirmation. Guide-#10 will reject incomplete close reports.
EA-10 stays on main throughout Phase 1. No branch checkout. No worktree creation.

Subagent verification per Ticket X / dn14 / A18 is OPTIONAL for Phase 1 (LOW risk; no destructive operations). Guide-#10 will spawn S1/S2/S3-style triple-subagent verification at Phase 3 (HIGH-priority architectural batch with II/TT/JJ) and at any future HIGH-risk phase.

Do NOT introduce file-output-pollution. The OO policy doc is the only NEW file created in Phase 1; all other Phase 1 work is edits to existing files (STATUS.md + CLAUDE.md) or Vikunja mutations (no file changes).
Do NOT delete or move Stage 5 close evidence (5.12-* + 5.13-* + soak_log.txt + soak_monitor.ps1 outputs) — those are committed audit-chain artifacts.
EA-10 may NOT spawn subagents during Phase 1. The retire-or-execute analysis was Guide-#10's one-pass; EA-10 executes the disposition without re-running the search.

If EA-10 detects context exhaustion mid-Phase-1, STOP at the current ticket completion (do NOT begin a new ticket), commit any pending edits, run Post-Flight unpause + commit, and report context-exhaustion to LA. Phase 1 will resume with EA-10-cycle-2 (or fresh EA-10') with the same dispatch text.

stage_6_phase_1_close_protocol.step: Author Phase 1 close report (free-form Markdown, not XML required) covering: 4 commit SHAs, 37 Vikunja mutation enumeration, readback evidence per ticket, Pre-Flight Step 0 outputs, Post-Flight unpause confirmation, observed anomalies (if any).
Submit close report to LA via chat.
LA forwards close report to Guide-#10 for cross-verification.
Guide-#10 issues Phase 1 close verdict (APPROVED / APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS / REJECTED).
If APPROVED: Phase 1 closes; LA may authorize Phase 2 dispatch (Items 6.4 + 6.5) at LA discretion.
If APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS: EA-10 (or fresh EA-10') executes follow-up corrections per Guide-#10 verdict before Phase 1 closes.
If REJECTED: Phase 1 reopens with corrected dispatch.

ea_init_pointer.a_note_on_la_calibration_review: Per established cycle pattern (Guide-#7 → #8 → #9 transitions): LA may submit EA-10's iter-1 comprehension-gate summary back to Guide-#9 (in standby) for calibration review BEFORE Guide-#10 issues a verdict. This is at LA discretion. Default path: LA submits EA-10's gate summary to Guide-#10 directly; Guide-#10 issues verdict; cycle proceeds. Guide-#9 standby is fallback only.
